About

Kim Dasha Gustavsson

AI Master’s student with a designer’s eye and a developer’s hands. A background in Informatics from Sweden — web, mobile, and design — now turned toward machine learning. I build intelligent systems and write about the work along the way.

Contact ↗

What I’m doing now

Building intelligent systems, end to end

As an AI Master’s student, I design and build small ML-driven tools from data to interface — owning the problem framing, the model, and the experience around it. Right now I’m experimenting with how these systems can stay genuinely useful and human-centered, not just technically impressive.

Bridging design and machine learning

My background is in Informatics — web, mobile, and design — so I tend to look at AI through a product lens: what does this actually do for a person? I write and share what I learn as I close the gap between the two.

How I work

Start from the real problem

Before building, I try to understand what’s actually being asked — talking to users, questioning assumptions, and reframing the problem until it’s the right one.

Think in structure

I sketch the flow and the data model before the screens. As more of the work gets automated, this underlying structure matters more than surface detail.

Iterate quickly

With the boundaries between design, data, and development blurring, I move fast — building, testing, and refining in tight loops.

What I work with

AI / ML

Python, PyTorch, scikit-learn, LLM APIs and prompt design, data pipelines, model evaluation, retrieval-augmented generation.

Design / Product

Figma, user research and interviews, problem framing, UX for web and mobile, turning ambiguous needs into scoped, shippable work.

Engineering

TypeScript, web and mobile development, working with APIs and databases, comfortable building and deploying small full-stack tools.

Communication

Translating technical work for non-experts, aligning teams on goals, and writing to think clearly in public.

Experience

2025 — present MSc in Artificial Intelligence Coursework, research, and independent ML projects
